topics.title: Certificate Lifecycle
topics.definition: The Certificate Lifecycle is the complete process a digital certificate follows from key generation until its secure destruction. Proper lifecycle management ensures certificates remain trusted, valid, and secure.
examPoints: The Certificate Lifecycle is the complete process a digital certificate follows from key generation until its secure destruction. Proper lifecycle management ensures certificates remain trusted, valid, and secure.
Every certificate follows the same lifecycle.
Poor lifecycle management leads to expired or compromised certificates.
topics.narration:
Certificate Lifecycle

The Certificate Lifecycle is the complete process a digital certificate follows from key generation until its secure destruction. Proper lifecycle management ensures certificates remain trusted, valid, and secure.

Important exam points. The Certificate Lifecycle is the complete process a digital certificate follows from key generation until its secure destruction. Proper lifecycle management ensures certificates remain trusted, valid, and secure.. Every certificate follows the same lifecycle.. Poor lifecycle management leads to expired or compromised certificates.

topics.title: Complete Certificate Lifecycle
workflows: Key Generation ↓ CSR Creation ↓ Identity Verification ↓ Certificate Issuance ↓ Distribution ↓ Installation ↓ Operational Use ↓ Renewal / Rekey ↓ Revocation / Expiration ↓ Destruction
topics.narration:
Complete Certificate Lifecycle

Workflow. Key Generation ↓ CSR Creation ↓ Identity Verification ↓ Certificate Issuance ↓ Distribution ↓ Installation ↓ Operational Use ↓ Renewal / Rekey ↓ Revocation / Expiration ↓ Destruction

topics.title: Key Generation
topics.definition: Key Generation is the process of creating a cryptographic Public-Private Key Pair using a secure algorithm.
examPoints: Key Generation is the process of creating a cryptographic Public-Private Key Pair using a secure algorithm.
topics.narration:
Key Generation

Key Generation is the process of creating a cryptographic Public-Private Key Pair using a secure algorithm.

Important exam points. Key Generation is the process of creating a cryptographic Public-Private Key Pair using a secure algorithm.. Private Key generated first.. Public Key derived mathematically.

topics.title: Random Number Generator (RNG)
topics.definition: RNG provides random values required to generate unpredictable cryptographic keys.
examPoints: RNG provides random values required to generate unpredictable cryptographic keys.
topics.narration:
Random Number Generator (RNG)

RNG provides random values required to generate unpredictable cryptographic keys.

Important exam points. RNG provides random values required to generate unpredictable cryptographic keys.. CSPRNG is preferred.. Weak randomness = Weak keys.

topics.title: RSA vs ECC Key Generation
comparisons: RSA: Large Keys; ECC: Small Keys
RSA: Widely Supported; ECC: Better Performance
topics.narration:
RSA vs ECC Key Generation

Comparison. RSA: Large Keys; ECC: Small Keys. RSA: Slower; ECC: Faster. RSA: Widely Supported; ECC: Better Performance

topics.title: Recommended Key Sizes
topics.narration:
Recommended Key Sizes

Important exam points. 2048. 3072. 4096. ECC. P-256. P-384. P-521

topics.title: CSR (Certificate Signing Request)
topics.definition: A CSR is a request generated by the applicant containing the Public Key and identity information, sent to the CA for certificate issuance.
examPoints: A CSR is a request generated by the applicant containing the Public Key and identity information, sent to the CA for certificate issuance.
topics.narration:
CSR (Certificate Signing Request)

A CSR is a request generated by the applicant containing the Public Key and identity information, sent to the CA for certificate issuance.

Important exam points. A CSR is a request generated by the applicant containing the Public Key and identity information, sent to the CA for certificate issuance.. Contains. Public Key. Subject. SAN. Never contains. Private Key

topics.title: CSR Verification
topics.definition: The CA verifies the CSR's signature and identity before issuing the certificate.
examPoints: The CA verifies the CSR's signature and identity before issuing the certificate.
topics.narration:
CSR Verification

The CA verifies the CSR's signature and identity before issuing the certificate.

Important exam points. The CA verifies the CSR's signature and identity before issuing the certificate.. Verifies. Applicant owns Private Key. Identity information

topics.title: CSR vs Certificate
comparisons: CSR: Applicant Signed; Certificate: CA Signed
topics.narration:
CSR vs Certificate

Comparison. CSR: Request; Certificate: Identity. CSR: Applicant Signed; Certificate: CA Signed

topics.title: Identity Verification
topics.definition: Identity Verification confirms that the applicant requesting a certificate is genuine.
examPoints: Identity Verification confirms that the applicant requesting a certificate is genuine.
topics.narration:
Identity Verification

Identity Verification confirms that the applicant requesting a certificate is genuine.

Important exam points. Identity Verification confirms that the applicant requesting a certificate is genuine.. Performed by. RA. CA

topics.title: Domain Validation (DV)
topics.definition: The CA verifies only domain ownership.
examPoints: The CA verifies only domain ownership.
topics.narration:
Domain Validation (DV)

The CA verifies only domain ownership.

Important exam points. The CA verifies only domain ownership.. Lowest validation level.. No organization verification.

topics.title: Organization Validation (OV)
topics.definition: The CA verifies both domain ownership and organization identity.
examPoints: The CA verifies both domain ownership and organization identity.
topics.narration:
Organization Validation (OV)

The CA verifies both domain ownership and organization identity.

Important exam points. The CA verifies both domain ownership and organization identity.. More trusted than DV.

topics.title: Extended Validation (EV)
topics.definition: The highest level of certificate validation requiring extensive legal and organizational verification.
examPoints: The highest level of certificate validation requiring extensive legal and organizational verification.
topics.narration:
Extended Validation (EV)

The highest level of certificate validation requiring extensive legal and organizational verification.

Important exam points. The highest level of certificate validation requiring extensive legal and organizational verification.. Highest Assurance. Most expensive

topics.title: DV vs OV vs EV
comparisons: DV: Domain; OV: Domain + Organization; EV: Extensive Legal Verification
DV: Fast; OV: Moderate; EV: Strict
topics.narration:
DV vs OV vs EV

Important exam points. OV → Organization. EV → Everything

Comparison. DV: Domain; OV: Domain + Organization; EV: Extensive Legal Verification. DV: Fast; OV: Moderate; EV: Strict

topics.title: Certificate Issuance
topics.definition: Certificate Issuance is the process where the CA signs the CSR using its private key to create a digital certificate.
examPoints: Certificate Issuance is the process where the CA signs the CSR using its private key to create a digital certificate.
topics.narration:
Certificate Issuance

Certificate Issuance is the process where the CA signs the CSR using its private key to create a digital certificate.

Important exam points. Certificate Issuance is the process where the CA signs the CSR using its private key to create a digital certificate.. CA adds. Serial Number. Validity. Issuer. Extensions

topics.title: Certificate Signing
topics.definition: The CA signs the TBSCertificate using its private key.
examPoints: The CA signs the TBSCertificate using its private key.
topics.narration:
Certificate Signing

The CA signs the TBSCertificate using its private key.

Important exam points. The CA signs the TBSCertificate using its private key.. Verification uses. CA Public Key

topics.title: Root vs Intermediate Issuance
examPoints: Root should NOT directly issue server certificates.
workflows: Root CA ↓ Intermediate CA ↓ Server Certificate
topics.narration:
Root vs Intermediate Issuance

Important exam points. Root should NOT directly issue server certificates.

Workflow. Root CA ↓ Intermediate CA ↓ Server Certificate

topics.title: Distribution
topics.definition: Distribution is the process of delivering certificates to servers, users, browsers, or applications.
examPoints: Distribution is the process of delivering certificates to servers, users, browsers, or applications.
topics.narration:
Distribution

Distribution is the process of delivering certificates to servers, users, browsers, or applications.

Important exam points. Distribution is the process of delivering certificates to servers, users, browsers, or applications.. LDAP. HTTP. Group Policy. MDM. ACME

topics.title: Certificate Repository
topics.definition: Stores certificates, CRLs, and CA certificates for retrieval.
examPoints: Stores certificates, CRLs, and CA certificates for retrieval.
topics.narration:
Certificate Repository

Stores certificates, CRLs, and CA certificates for retrieval.

Important exam points. Stores certificates, CRLs, and CA certificates for retrieval.. LDAP. HTTP

topics.title: Installation
topics.definition: Installation places the certificate and private key into the application or server.
examPoints: Installation places the certificate and private key into the application or server.
topics.narration:
Installation

Installation places the certificate and private key into the application or server.

Important exam points. Installation places the certificate and private key into the application or server.. Server Requires. Certificate. Private Key. Intermediate Chain

topics.title: Browser Installation
topics.definition: Root CA certificates are installed into browser or operating system trust stores.
examPoints: Root CA certificates are installed into browser or operating system trust stores.
Makes browser trust certificates issued by that CA.
topics.narration:
Browser Installation

Root CA certificates are installed into browser or operating system trust stores.

Important exam points. Root CA certificates are installed into browser or operating system trust stores.. Makes browser trust certificates issued by that CA.

topics.title: Apache Installation
topics.narration:
Apache Installation

Important exam points. Private Key. Full Chain

topics.title: Nginx Installation
topics.narration:
Nginx Installation

Commands. Uses: ssl_certificate ssl_certificate_key

topics.title: Operational Use
topics.definition: The certificate is actively used for TLS, authentication, encryption, or digital signatures.
examPoints: The certificate is actively used for TLS, authentication, encryption, or digital signatures.
topics.narration:
Operational Use

The certificate is actively used for TLS, authentication, encryption, or digital signatures.

Important exam points. The certificate is actively used for TLS, authentication, encryption, or digital signatures.. HTTPS. VPN. Email. Code Signing

topics.title: Renewal
topics.definition: Renewal replaces an expiring certificate while optionally reusing the existing key pair.
examPoints: Renewal replaces an expiring certificate while optionally reusing the existing key pair.
topics.narration:
Renewal

Renewal replaces an expiring certificate while optionally reusing the existing key pair.

Important exam points. Renewal replaces an expiring certificate while optionally reusing the existing key pair.. New Certificate. Same Key (Optional)

topics.title: Automatic Renewal
topics.definition: Renewal performed automatically using tools like ACME or enterprise certificate managers.
examPoints: Renewal performed automatically using tools like ACME or enterprise certificate managers.
topics.narration:
Automatic Renewal

Renewal performed automatically using tools like ACME or enterprise certificate managers.

Important exam points. Renewal performed automatically using tools like ACME or enterprise certificate managers.. Let's Encrypt

topics.title: Rekey
topics.definition: Rekey generates a completely new key pair and issues a new certificate.
examPoints: Rekey generates a completely new key pair and issues a new certificate.
topics.narration:
Rekey

Rekey generates a completely new key pair and issues a new certificate.

Important exam points. Rekey generates a completely new key pair and issues a new certificate.. Always. New Key. New Certificate

topics.title: Renewal vs Rekey
comparisons: Renewal: Same Key Possible; Rekey: New Key Mandatory
Renewal: New Certificate; Rekey: New Certificate
topics.narration:
Renewal vs Rekey

Important exam points. Rekey = Replace Keys

Comparison. Renewal: Same Key Possible; Rekey: New Key Mandatory. Renewal: New Certificate; Rekey: New Certificate

topics.title: Revocation
topics.definition: Revocation invalidates a certificate before its expiration date.
examPoints: Revocation invalidates a certificate before its expiration date.
topics.narration:
Revocation

Revocation invalidates a certificate before its expiration date.

Important exam points. Revocation invalidates a certificate before its expiration date.. Reasons. Key Compromise. Employee Left. Wrong Certificate

topics.title: Revocation Methods
topics.narration:
Revocation Methods

Important exam points. OCSP

topics.title: Expiration
topics.definition: Expiration occurs automatically when the certificate reaches its Not After date.
examPoints: Expiration occurs automatically when the certificate reaches its Not After date.
topics.narration:
Expiration

Expiration occurs automatically when the certificate reaches its Not After date.

Important exam points. Expiration occurs automatically when the certificate reaches its Not After date.. Automatic. Time Based

topics.title: Expired vs Revoked
topics.narration:
Expired vs Revoked

Comparison. Expired: Automatic; Revoked: Manual. Expired: Planned; Revoked: Emergency

topics.title: Destruction
topics.definition: Securely deleting or destroying certificates and especially private keys after they are no longer needed.
examPoints: Securely deleting or destroying certificates and especially private keys after they are no longer needed.
topics.narration:
Destruction

Securely deleting or destroying certificates and especially private keys after they are no longer needed.

Important exam points. Securely deleting or destroying certificates and especially private keys after they are no longer needed.. Destroy. Private Keys. Backup Copies

topics.title: HSM (Hardware Security Module)
topics.definition: A specialized hardware device that securely generates, stores, and protects cryptographic keys.
examPoints: A specialized hardware device that securely generates, stores, and protects cryptographic keys.
Best protection for CA Private Keys.
topics.narration:
HSM (Hardware Security Module)

A specialized hardware device that securely generates, stores, and protects cryptographic keys.

Important exam points. A specialized hardware device that securely generates, stores, and protects cryptographic keys.. Best protection for CA Private Keys.

topics.title: Backup Removal
topics.definition: Private keys must also be removed from backups after retirement.
examPoints: Private keys must also be removed from backups after retirement.
Deleting only the server copy is insufficient.
topics.narration:
Backup Removal

Private keys must also be removed from backups after retirement.

Important exam points. Private keys must also be removed from backups after retirement.. Deleting only the server copy is insufficient.

topics.title: Compliance
topics.narration:
Compliance

Important exam points. ISO 27001. PCI DSS. HIPAA. GDPR

topics.title: OpenSSL Commands
topics.narration:
OpenSSL Commands

Commands. Generate Key: openssl genpkey. Create CSR: openssl req -new. Issue Certificate: openssl x509 -req. Check Dates: openssl x509 -dates. Verify Certificate: openssl verify

topics.title: Enterprise Certificate Management
topics.narration:
Enterprise Certificate Management

Important exam points. Certificate Discovery. Renewal. Monitoring. Revocation. Automation

topics.title: Renewal vs Rekey
comparisons: Renewal: Same Key Allowed; Rekey: New Key Pair
Renewal: Replace Certificate; Rekey: Replace Keys + Certificate
topics.narration:
Renewal vs Rekey

Comparison. Renewal: Same Key Allowed; Rekey: New Key Pair. Renewal: Replace Certificate; Rekey: Replace Keys + Certificate

topics.title: DV vs OV vs EV
comparisons: DV: Domain; OV: Organization; EV: Highest Validation
topics.narration:
DV vs OV vs EV

Comparison. DV: Domain; OV: Organization; EV: Highest Validation

topics.title: Root vs Intermediate
comparisons: Root: Signs Intermediate; Intermediate: Signs End Certificates
topics.narration:
Root vs Intermediate

Comparison. Root: Offline; Intermediate: Online. Root: Signs Intermediate; Intermediate: Signs End Certificates

topics.title: Revocation vs Expiration
comparisons: Revocation: Security Event; Expiration: Time Limit
topics.narration:
Revocation vs Expiration

Comparison. Revocation: Manual; Expiration: Automatic. Revocation: Security Event; Expiration: Time Limit

topics.title: Complete Lifecycle Workflow (Very Important)
workflows: Generate Key Pair ↓ Create CSR ↓ RA Verifies Identity ↓ CA Issues Certificate ↓ Distribute Certificate ↓ Install Certificate ↓ Use Certificate ↓ Renew OR Rekey ↓ Revoke if Needed ↓ Expire ↓ Destroy Keys
topics.narration:
Complete Lifecycle Workflow (Very Important)

Workflow. Generate Key Pair ↓ Create CSR ↓ RA Verifies Identity ↓ CA Issues Certificate ↓ Distribute Certificate ↓ Install Certificate ↓ Use Certificate ↓ Renew OR Rekey ↓ Revoke if Needed ↓ Expire ↓ Destroy Keys

topics.title: Top 30 One-Liners for Exam
oneLiners: Certificate Lifecycle begins with Key Generation.
Private Key is generated before the Public Key.
Public Key is derived from the Private Key.
CSR contains the Public Key, not the Private Key.
CSR is signed by the applicant.
Identity verification is performed by the RA or CA.
DV verifies domain ownership only.
OV verifies organization and domain.
EV provides the highest level of validation.
CA signs the certificate using its Private Key.
Clients verify the certificate using the CA Public Key.
Root CA signs Intermediate CA certificates.
Intermediate CA signs server certificates.
Certificates are distributed through LDAP, HTTP, GPO, MDM, or ACME.
A server needs the certificate, private key, and intermediate chain.
Root CA certificates belong in the trust store.
Renewal replaces a certificate.
Rekey replaces both the certificate and key pair.
Rekey is recommended after key compromise.
Revocation happens before expiration.
Expiration occurs at the Not After date.
CRL and OCSP communicate revocation status.
Destroy the private key, not just the certificate.
Remove retired private keys from backups as well.
HSMs provide the highest protection for CA keys.
Automation reduces certificate-expiry outages.
Monitor all certificates before they expire.
Never allow an expired certificate to remain in production.
Lifecycle management is essential for PKI security and compliance.
The full lifecycle to memorize is: Key Generation → CSR → Verification → Issuance → Distribution → Installation → Use → Renewal/Rekey → Revocation/Expiration → Destruction.
topics.narration:

CSR contains the Public Key, not the Private Key.. CSR is signed by the applicant.. Identity verification is performed by the RA or CA.. DV verifies domain ownership only.. OV verifies organization and domain.. EV provides the highest level of validation.. CA signs the certificate using its Private Key.. Clients verify the certificate using the CA Public Key.. Root CA signs Intermediate CA certificates.. Intermediate CA signs server certificates.. Certificates are distributed through LDAP, HTTP, GPO, MDM, or ACME.. A server needs the certificate, private key, and intermediate chain.. Root CA certificates belong in the trust store.. Renewal replaces a certificate.. Rekey replaces both the certificate and key pair.. Rekey is recommended after key compromise.. Revocation happens before expiration.. Expiration occurs at the Not After date.. CRL and OCSP communicate revocation status.. Destroy the private key, not just the certificate.. Remove retired private keys from backups as well.. HSMs provide the highest protection for CA keys.. Automation reduces certificate-expiry outages.. Monitor all certificates before they expire.. Never allow an expired certificate to remain in production.. Lifecycle management is essential for PKI security and compliance.. The full lifecycle to memorize is: Key Generation → CSR → Verification → Issuance → Distribution → Installation → Use → Renewal/Rekey → Revocation/Expiration → Destruction.
